name: Zirconia Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Zirconia. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Zirconia surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength with good absorption in Zirconia for efficient cleaning

spotSize.description: Optimal beam spot diameter for precision cleaning of Zirconia surfaces

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage while maintaining thermal control

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal from Zirconia

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with minimal substrate damage

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning without excessive heat accumulation

power.description: Typical range for industrial Nd:YAG laser cleaning of ceramics like Zirconia to remove contaminants without thermal damage; starting at 150 W for controlled ablation. Max safe ~500 W to avoid cracking due to low thermal conductivity. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Journal of Laser Applications (2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for 1064 nm cleaning of Zirconia; slower for heavy contamination (200-800 mm/s), faster for light (1500-3000 mm/s) to minimize heat input. Recommended 1000 mm/s for balanced removal and surface integrity. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Optics Express (2021).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for industrial cleaning of Zirconia to balance efficiency and minimal HAZ; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier applications. 50 ns recommended to limit thermal diffusion in low-conductivity Zirconia. Source: Laser physics review in Applied Surface Science (2019).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 40 kHz recommended for Zirconia to achieve fast processing without excessive heat accumulation, given its high melting point but low thermal conductivity. Higher rates (>80 kHz) risk overheating. Source: Academic literature in Journal of Applied Physics (2020).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal on Zirconia; damage threshold ~4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity and absorption properties. 1.5 J/cm² recommended for safe cleaning without substrate ablation. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; smaller (100-200 μm) for fine surface prep, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. 200 μm recommended for Zirconia to ensure uniform energy distribution and avoid localized overheating. Source: Manufacturer specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete Zirconia cleaning while preserving integrity, based on iterative removal without cumulative damage. Source: Industry case studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (2022).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage in scan patterns; 70% recommended for Zirconia to achieve even cleaning without gaps or overexposure, optimizing for its brittle nature. Typical range 50-80% in pulsed laser processes. Source: Process optimization in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2019).
